{{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | Group = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | Coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | Group = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | Coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | Group = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | Coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:ashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun}} Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | Group = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label Position = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | Group = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label Position = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | Group = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like shape. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various typed haniwa, such as house-typed, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

Height 3m (rear circular part) | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == Burial Facility == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Cultural Properties == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == other_designation == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == other_designation == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | Alias = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (designation name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == other_designation == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The artifacts are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town other_designation. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and artifacts in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's dimensions are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == other_designation == * Mashiko Town Designated Cultural Property: ** other_designation: Tennozuka Kofun (designated February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays artifacts from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated other_designations in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods.
