{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}[[Category:11]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]]

The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}

The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=draft}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=draft}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | Excavated Items = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=draft|12=draft}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == Excavated Items == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. Exhibited at the Aizu Yaich ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{draft categories| |12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Mashiko]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[Category:Search started pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[en:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[sn:ashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun}}

(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=draft|12=draft}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=draft|12=draft}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. revision.comment: 8 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:益子天王塚古墳 up to rev 99995253
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 益子天王塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に後円部・石室開口部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = 荒久台1号墳 |所属 = 荒久台古墳群 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]益子（字荒久台） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 20.90 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 30.30 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長43m
高さ3m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 銅鏡・[[装飾付大刀]]ほか副葬品多数・[[土師器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 益子町指定史跡「天王塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 益子
天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''益子天王塚古墳'''（ましこてんのうづかこふん、荒久台1号墳）は、[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]益子にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。荒久台古墳群（あらくだいこふんぐん）を構成する古墳の1つ。益子町指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「天王塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 栃木県南東部、八溝山地西端の丘陵上（標高約111メートル）に築造された古墳である。丘陵上には古墳28基（[[前方後円墳]]1基（本古墳）・[[円墳]]28基、現存18基）が分布して荒久台古墳群を形成し、本古墳はその主墳になる{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。[[1954年|1954]]-[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]29-30年）に調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西南西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・人物・馬形・鶏形・鈴形埴輪など）が認められる。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。埋葬施設は後円部における両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室内の調査では、副葬品として銅鏡・[[装飾付大刀]]のほか装身具・大刀・甲冑・馬具など多数が検出されている。質・量とも優れた資料群であり、保存状態も良好であるとして注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|天王塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。豊富な優品を副葬品とする点で特色を示し、栃木県における後期古墳の代表例として重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|天王塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に益子町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1954年|1954]]-[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]29-30年） ** 発掘調査：計3次（滝口宏ら[[早稲田大学]]考古学研究室）{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 ** 墳丘・周溝・石室実測調査（久保哲三ら[[宇都宮大学]]考古学研究会・益子町教育委員会、[[1956年]]に概要報告）{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）2月7日、益子町指定史跡に指定。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年）、石室東壁の一部補修（益子町教育委員会）{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、『益子町史』第1巻で調査概要・出土資料の公表{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * 墳丘長：43メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約21メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約26メートル ** 高さ：約2.2メートル 墳丘外表では、墳裾から幅3メートルの範囲で扁平な山石を用いた[[葺石]]が施される。葺石の上下には3メートルに5本程度の割合（一定間隔ではない）の[[円筒埴輪]]列が認められ、北側くびれ部付近では朝顔形埴輪、後円部墳頂では家形埴輪2個体、前方部墳頂付近では人物・馬形埴輪、前方部周溝では鶏形・馬形・鈴形埴輪が出土している。また南側くびれ部前方部寄りの円筒埴輪列の間では底部穿孔のない土師器壺が検出されている{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 墳丘周囲には墳丘と同形態で幅4-5メートルの周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては後円部において両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、墳丘主軸に直交して南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * 石室全長：6.85メートル * 玄室：長さ4.55メートル、幅1.57メートル（中央）、高さ1.61メートル * 羨道：長さ2.30メートル 石室の石材は硬砂岩で、玄室は割石の小口積みによって構築される。奥壁は1枚石。床面には小砂利を厚さ10センチメートル敷いた上に、薄い板石を並べる。天井石は8石（墳頂下2.2メートル）で、その上を厚さ約20センチメートルの粘土で屋根形に被覆する。玄室と玄門の間では、2本の玄門柱・楣石によって幅0.6メートルの玄門を形成する。羨道は2段構造で、下段の床面は玄室と同じ高さであるが、上段は0.85メートル上の高さであり、上段部分が閉塞部となる。閉塞部は、羨道上段・下段の境に板石を立て、上段側に川原石を75個積んで隙間を粘土で目張りし、羨門部を粘土・鹿沼土で密封して形成する{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:益子天王塚古墳出土 単鳳環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|単鳳環頭大刀柄頭}}{{small|[[早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館]]展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[衝角付冑]]}}]] 1954-1955年（昭和29-30年）の調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}{{Sfn|益子天王塚古墳の時代|2005}}。 * 銅鏡 1 - 仿製六獣鏡（直径13.8センチメートル）。 * 木棺金具 2 * 装身具 ** 金環 ** 琥珀製勾玉 1 ** 滑石製管玉 1 ** ガラス製小玉 約100 * 武器・武具類 ** 大刀 3 - 単鳳環頭大刀1。 ** 鏃 約150 ** 刀子 4 ** [[衝角付冑]] 1 ** 挂甲 1 * 馬具 一式 ** 轡 ** 鞍金具 ** 壺鐙 ** 辻金具 ** 杏葉 ** 馬鈴 ファイル:益子天王塚古墳出土 武器・武具.JPG|武器・武具 ファイル:益子天王塚古墳出土 馬具.JPG|馬具 == 文化財 == === 益子町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 天王塚古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）2月7日指定[https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/cultural_property.php?mode=detail&code=100 天王塚古墳]（益子町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館]]（東京都新宿区） - 益子天王塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1987|chapter=荒久台古墳群|title=益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編|publisher=益子町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=天王塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|天王塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本澄朗|year=1989|chapter=天王塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|天王塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=早稲田大学文学学術院考古学研究室|author=|year=2005|chapter=天王塚古墳|title=益子天王塚古墳の時代 図録|publisher=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|益子天王塚古墳の時代|2005}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=久保哲三|title=下野益子天王塚古墳調査予報|date=1956|journal=古代|publisher=早稲田大学考古学会|volume=|number=18|pages=13-17|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=山田俊輔・米澤雅美・土屋隆史|title=益子天王塚古墳出土遺物の調査 円筒埴輪・鉄鏃|date=2007|journal=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館研究紀要|publisher=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|volume=|number=9|pages=75-91|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=持田大輔・中條英樹|title=益子天王塚古墳出土遺物の調査（2） 環頭大刀・馬具|date=2008|journal=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館研究紀要|publisher=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|volume=|number=10|pages=67-89|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=山田琴子・持田大輔|title=益子天王塚古墳出土遺物の調査（3） 衝角付冑|date=2009|journal=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館研究紀要|publisher=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|volume=|number=11|pages=97-111|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=青木弘・持田大輔|title=益子天王塚古墳出土遺物の調査（4） 人物埴輪|date=2010|journal=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館研究紀要|publisher=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|volume=|number=12|pages=133-152|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=山田琴子|title=益子天王塚古墳出土遺物の調査（5） 挂甲|date=2011|journal=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館研究紀要|publisher=早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|volume=|number=13|pages=135-149|url=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun}} * revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[益子天王塚古墳]] to [[Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 益子天王塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に後円部・石室開口部、左奥に前方部） |別名 = 荒久台1号墳 |所属 = 荒久台古墳群 |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]益子（字荒久台） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 20.90 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 6|経度秒 = 30.30 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長43m
高さ3m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 銅鏡・[[装飾付大刀]]ほか副葬品多数・[[土師器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 益子町指定史跡「天王塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 益子
天王塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''益子天王塚古墳'''（ましこてんのうづかこふん、荒久台1号墳）は、[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]益子にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。荒久台古墳群（あらくだいこふんぐん）を構成する古墳の1つ。益子町指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「天王塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 栃木県南東部、八溝山地西端の丘陵上（標高約111メートル）に築造された古墳である。丘陵上には古墳28基（[[前方後円墳]]1基（本古墳）・[[円墳]]28基、現存18基）が分布して荒久台古墳群を形成し、本古墳はその主墳になる{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。[[1954年|1954]]-[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]29-30年）に調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西南西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（家形・人物・馬形・鶏形・鈴形埴輪など）が認められる。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。埋葬施設は後円部における両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室内の調査では、副葬品として銅鏡・[[装飾付大刀]]のほか装身具・大刀・甲冑・馬具など多数が検出されている。質・量とも優れた資料群であり、保存状態も良好であるとして注目される。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|天王塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。豊富な優品を副葬品とする点で特色を示し、栃木県における後期古墳の代表例として重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|天王塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（昭和48年）に益子町指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1954年|1954]]-[[1955年]]（[[昭和]]29-30年） ** 発掘調査：計3次（滝口宏ら[[早稲田大学]]考古学研究室）{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 ** 墳丘・周溝・石室実測調査（久保哲三ら[[宇都宮大学]]考古学研究会・益子町教育委員会、[[1956年]]に概要報告）{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * [[1973年]]（昭和48年）2月7日、益子町指定史跡に指定。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年）、石室東壁の一部補修（益子町教育委員会）{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）、『益子町史』第1巻で調査概要・出土資料の公表{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * 墳丘長：43メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：約21メートル ** 高さ：約3メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約26メートル ** 高さ：約2.2メートル 墳丘外表では、墳裾から幅3メートルの範囲で扁平な山石を用いた[[葺石]]が施される。葺石の上下には3メートルに5本程度の割合（一定間隔ではない）の[[円筒埴輪]]列が認められ、北側くびれ部付近では朝顔形埴輪、後円部墳頂では家形埴輪2個体、前方部墳頂付近では人物・馬形埴輪、前方部周溝では鶏形・馬形・鈴形埴輪が出土している。また南側くびれ部前方部寄りの円筒埴輪列の間では底部穿孔のない土師器壺が検出されている{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 墳丘周囲には墳丘と同形態で幅4-5メートルの周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては後円部において両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、墳丘主軸に直交して南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 * 石室全長：6.85メートル * 玄室：長さ4.55メートル、幅1.57メートル（中央）、高さ1.61メートル * 羨道：長さ2.30メートル 石室の石材は硬砂岩で、玄室は割石の小口積みによって構築される。奥壁は1枚石。床面には小砂利を厚さ10センチメートル敷いた上に、薄い板石を並べる。天井石は8石（墳頂下2.2メートル）で、その上を厚さ約20センチメートルの粘土で屋根形に被覆する。玄室と玄門の間では、2本の玄門柱・楣石によって幅0.6メートルの玄門を形成する。羨道は2段構造で、下段の床面は玄室と同じ高さであるが、上段は0.85メートル上の高さであり、上段部分が閉塞部となる。閉塞部は、羨道上段・下段の境に板石を立て、上段側に川原石を75個積んで隙間を粘土で目張りし、羨門部を粘土・鹿沼土で密封して形成する{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}。 File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） File:Mashiko Tennozuka 1954-1955年（昭和29-30年）の調査で石室内から検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|荒久台古墳群（益子町史 第1巻 考古資料編）|1987}}{{Sfn|益子天王塚古墳の時代|2005}}。 * 銅鏡 1 - 仿製六獣鏡（直径13.8センチメートル）。 * 木棺金具 2 * 装身具 ** 金環 ** 琥珀製勾玉 1 ** 滑石製管玉 1 ** ガラス製小玉 約100 * 武器・武具類 ** 大刀 3 - 単鳳環頭大刀1。 ** 鏃 約150 ** 刀子 4 ** [[衝角付冑]] 1 ** 挂甲 1 * 馬具 一式 ** 轡 ** 鞍金具 ** 壺鐙 ** 辻金具 ** 杏葉 ** 馬鈴 ファイル:益子天王塚古墳出土 武器・武具.JPG|武器・武具 ファイル:益子天王塚古墳出土 馬具.JPG|馬具 == 文化財 == === 益子町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 天王塚古墳 Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}

(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=draft|12=draft}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=draft|12=draft}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haga District|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. (Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haga District|qq=}}{{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko|qq=}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{coord|36|28|20.90|N|140|6|30.30|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated sword|qq=}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haga District|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|qq=}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|qq=}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. Exhibited at the Aizu Yaich ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|益子天王塚古墳|version=99995253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[en:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[sn:ashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]]

Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mashiko|ja|益子町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Haga District|ja|芳賀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haga District|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|qq=}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/6th century|qq=}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|qq=}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. Exhibited at the Aizu Yaich ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|益子天王塚古墳|version=99995253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[sn:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[en:Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]] [[sn:ashiko Tennōzuka Kofun]]
