Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District]][[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko]] Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{Coord|36.47247222222222|N|140.10841666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated sword|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48749737}}, numerous grave goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko]], [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}

Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko]], [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}

[[co:commons:Category:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun]][[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun]][[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun]][[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116693871]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District]][[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko]] Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{Coord|36.47247222222222|N|140.10841666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated sword|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48749737}}, numerous grave goods, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Mashiko, Tochigi|Mashiko]], [[:en:Haga District, Tochigi|Haga District]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. Exhibited at the Aizu Yaich ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|益子天王塚古墳|version=99995253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=draft}} [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q116693871}} [[Category:History of Mashiko Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}

Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated sword|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48749737}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|WD=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko}}, {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|en|Haga District, Tochigi|WD=Q1192087|lt=Haga District}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}
{{small|Mashiko Town, Tochigi Prefecture. Exhibited at the Aizu Yaich ... 13 KB (1,542 words) - 07:20, 19 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|益子天王塚古墳|version=99995253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=draft}} [[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q116693871}} [[Category:History of Mashiko Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}

[[co:commons:Category:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun]][[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun]][[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun]][[ja:益子天王塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound
(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|en|Haga District, Tochigi|WD=Q1192087|lt=Haga District|1=Haga District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|WD=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko|1=Mashiko, Tochigi}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{Coord|36.47247222222222|N|140.10841666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated sword|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48749737}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|WD=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko|1=Mashiko, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|en|Haga District, Tochigi|WD=Q1192087|lt=Haga District|1=Haga District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}

Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}

(Rear circular part with stone chamber opening on the right, front part in the back left) | alternate_name = Arakudai No.1 Kofun | part_of = Arakudai Kofun Group | location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|en|Haga District, Tochigi|qid=Q1192087|lt=Haga District|1=Haga District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|qid=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko|1=Mashiko, Tochigi}} Mashiko (Arakudai) | coordinates = {{Coord|36.47247222222222|N|140.10841666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} | area = Mound length 43m
Height 3m (rear circular part) | burial = Dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Bronze mirror, {{ill|decorated sword|ja|装飾付大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decorated sword|1=decorated swords|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48749737}}, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} | Historic Site = Mashiko Town Designated Historic Site "Tennozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Mashiko
Tennozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun''' (益子天王塚古墳), also known as Arakudai No.1 Kofun, is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mashiko, Tochigi|en|Mashiko, Tochigi|qid=Q1346925|lt=Mashiko|1=Mashiko, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Haga District, Tochigi|en|Haga District, Tochigi|qid=Q1192087|lt=Haga District|1=Haga District, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} and one of the tumuli that make up the Arakudai Kofun Group. It has been Historic Site1 as a Mashiko Town Historic Site (Historic Site name: "Tennozuka Kofun"). == Overview == This kofun is constructed on a hill (elevation approximately 111 meters) at the western edge of the {{ill|Yamizo Mountains|ja|八溝山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamizo Mountains|1=Yamizo Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391525}} in southeastern Tochigi Prefecture. The Arakudai Kofun Group consists of 28 tumuli (1 keyhole-shaped tumulus—this kofun—and 27 round tumuli, with 18 still existing). This kofun is the main tumulus of the group. Excavations were conducted between 1954 and 1955. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with the front facing west-southwest. The surface features include {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and rows of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, including morning glory-shaped haniwa, and various shaped haniwa (house-shaped, human figures, horse-shaped, chicken-shaped, bell-shaped). A surrounding moat encircles the mound. The burial facility is a dual-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the rear circular part, opening to the south. Numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors, decorated swords, ornaments, armor, and horse gear, were discovered inside. The burial goods are notable for their quality, quantity, and excellent preservation. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It is considered an important example of a late Kofun period burial in Tochigi Prefecture. == History == * 1954-1955: Excavation conducted by Waseda University Archaeology Laboratory. * 1973 (February 7): Designated as a Mashiko Town Historic Site. * 1982: Partial restoration of the east wall of the stone chamber. * 1987: Publication of excavation reports and burial goods in "Mashiko Town History" Volume 1. == Mound area == * Total length: 43 meters * Rear circular part: Diameter approx. 21 meters, Height approx. 3 meters * Front part: Width approx. 26 meters, Height approx. 2.2 meters The mound is covered with fukiishi, and rows of cylindrical haniwa are placed around it. Various shaped haniwa, such as house-shaped, human, horse, and chicken figures, were found. == burial == The stone chamber is a dual-sleeved yokoana-style stone chamber, oriented south. The chamber's area are: * Total length: 6.85 meters * Main chamber: Length 4.55m, Width 1.57m, Height 1.61m * Entrance passage: Length 2.30m The stone materials are hard sandstone, with large single stones used for the rear wall. The floor consists of small gravel and thin stone slabs. The ceiling consists of eight stones, covered with clay in a roof-like type. == burial goods == * Bronze mirror (replica six-beast mirror) * Decorated swords (including one single-phoenix ring pommel sword) * Ornaments: gold rings, amber magatama, steatite beads, glass beads * Weapons: swords, arrowheads, daggers * Armor: kabuto (helmet), armor plates * Horse gear: bits, saddle fittings, stirrups, decorative fittings, bells == Historic Site == * Mashiko Town Designated designation1: ** Historic Site: Tennozuka Kofun (Historic Site1 February 7, 1973) == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|ja|早稲田大学會津八一記念博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waseda University Aizu Yaichi Memorial Museum|1=Waseda Aizu Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30938116}} (Tokyo) - Displays burial goods from Mashiko Tennozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.town.mashiko.lg.jp/notes.php?mode=detail&code=100 Tennozuka Kofun - Mashiko Town Website] * [[Horned Kabuto]] 益子天王塚古墳出土 衝角付冑.JPG|Horned helmet from {{ill|Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mashiko Tennōzuka Kofun|qq=}}
